name: Story Builder
description: Create fictional worlds with characters, relationships, narrative arcs, factions, lore, and rules. AI-powered scene and chapter generation uses all dimensions for consistency.
author: ultralight

create_world.description: Create a new fictional world with a name, genre, and description.

add_character.description: Add a character to a world with traits, backstory, and role.

role.description: Role in the story (e.g. protagonist, antagonist, mentor)

add_setting.description: Add a location or setting to a world.

add_theme.description: Add a thematic element to a world (e.g. redemption, power, identity).

description.description: How this theme manifests in the story

add_relationship.description: Define a relationship between two characters (e.g. rivalry, alliance, mentor-student, roommates). Relationships are the dramatic engine — they shape how characters interact in generated scenes.

type.description: Relationship type (e.g. rivals, allies, mentor-student, roommates, enemies, uneasy-truce, transactional)

description.description: How this relationship works — dynamics, tension, history

add_arc.description: Add a narrative arc or timeline event — tracks character journeys, season structure, and story progression so the AI knows where characters are in their development.

name.description: Arc name (e.g. 'Snow B's Rise', 'The Earl Revelation')

description.description: What happens in this arc — beginning, middle, end state

add_faction.description: Add a political faction, alliance, or social group with its member characters. Factions shape the political topology of scenes.

description.description: Faction's goals, ideology, and position in the world

member_ids.description: Character IDs who belong to this faction

add_lore.description: Add in-universe lore: institutions, media properties, cultural artifacts, historical events, or world-building details that characters reference or interact with.

description.description: What this is, how it works, and why it matters to the world

add_rule.description: Add a world rule or craft constraint — things the AI must respect when generating content. Use for scientific accuracy requirements, narrative tone rules, or any hard constraint on generated output.

name.description: Rule name (e.g. 'Scientific Accuracy', 'No Breaking Fourth Wall')

description.description: The rule and how it should be applied

generate.description: Generate new content (scene, chapter, dialogue, outline) using AI with full world context including all characters, relationships, factions, arcs, lore, rules, settings, themes, and recent scenes.

prompt.description: Creative direction (e.g. 'A confrontation between X and Y at the castle')

character_ids.description: Focus on specific characters — their relationships will be highlighted in context

returns.description: Generated content with scene ID if saved

status.description: Overview of all worlds with counts for every dimension: characters, settings, themes, scenes, relationships, arcs, factions, lore, and rules.
